column61: Any creature that starts its turn within 50 feet of Temporis must succeed on a DC 21 Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes 4d10 psychic damage and has its speed halved until the start of its next turn as it experiences temporal displacement. On a successful save, the creature takes half damage and suffers no other effects.
column62:
When Temporis is reduced to 0 hit points, the temporal wound it embodies begins to unravel. The following effects occur:
- A rift in space-time opens in Temporis's space
- Each creature within 100 feet must succeed on a DC 18 Constitution saving throw
- On a failed save, a creature takes 10d10 psychic damage and is moved 1d4 × 10 feet in a random direction as time distorts around them
- On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and isn't moved
- The area within 1000 feet becomes a temporary temporal anomaly zone for 24 hours, where:
    - Time moves unpredictably
    - Creatures that end their turn in the zone must make a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or experience a random temporal effect
    - The Lorekeepers may be drawn to the area to contain the anomaly

Some astronomers believe these Pokémon can navigate by the stars with perfect accuracy, regardless of cloud cover.

The minerals in their diet determine the color and properties of their horns, with specimens from different mountain ranges displaying unique variations. Battles between males involve impressive horn displays rather than direct combat, creating dazzling light shows visible from valleys below.

column4: Dwelling in the deepest polluted waters, Horrish have adapted to thrive in environments toxic to most life. Their translucent bodies pulse with an eerie light that reveals the skeletal structure beneath, while toxic-absorbing organs glow with the pollutants they've consumed. They travel in small schools that can purify remarkable volumes of water, leaving cleansed patches in even the most contaminated environments. Ancient sailors believed Horrish appeared in waters that had been tainted by human sin, their ghostly forms representing departed spirits come to cleanse wrongdoing.

column4: This ancient being exists simultaneously in the material world and the realm of dreams. Once every millennium, when specific stars align, Jirachi awakens fully for seven days. During this time, it can reshape reality according to the wishes of those it deems worthy. The third eye upon its body is said to see not what is, but what could be. Scholars debate whether Jirachi grants wishes through cosmic power or simply glimpses potential futures and guides reality toward them.

column4: An entity born when cosmic radiation from distant stars struck raw ether in the Void between planes. Deoxys constantly reconfigures its cellular structure, allowing it to adapt instantly to any environment or threat. Scholars theorize it is not truly a Pokémon but rather cosmic energy that has attained consciousness and self-determination. Its various forms represent different solutions to the question of existence, each optimized for different purposes. Deoxys appears most often following meteor showers, suggesting it uses these celestial events to travel between planes.

column4: This enigmatic crystalline entity emerges from meteorites that have traversed the Void between planes. Protoxys absorbs cosmic radiation, electrical energy, and ether currents from its surroundings, using this energy to gradually develop its form. Those who have encountered these rare beings report telepathic impressions of curiosity and a profound sense of 'becoming.' Scientists theorize that Protoxys represents the developmental stage of something far more complex, adapting to existence in our plane of reality before achieving its final form.

column4:
Smudgel are commonly found in urban areas across the Shiman Federation, particularly in cities like Venustia, Astria, and Inex. These diligent Pokémon have developed a unique relationship with human society, serving as invaluable assistants in maintenance and cleaning work. Unlike their artistic cousin Smeargle, Smudgel's tail secretes a special cleaning solution that can break down most common stains and grime.
Their brown and gray coloration helps them blend in with work environments, and the white patches on their bodies often become stained from their labors - a badge of honor among hardworking Smudgel. They're frequently employed by businesses, municipal services, and even noble houses to maintain cleanliness in large facilities.
column60: Once per day, this Pokémon can use its action to remove all mundane dirt, grime, and stains from a 10-foot radius area. This ability can also neutralize Poison-type residue and removes the Poisoned condition from allies in range.
column61: This Pokémon has advantage on Constitution saving throws against exhaustion from manual labor. Can work twice as long as other Pokémon before requiring rest.
column62: The fluid from This Pokémon's tail deals +1d4 damage against Poison and Steel-type Pokémon. The damage dice for this ability increase to 1d6 at level 5, 2d6 at level 10, and 2d8 at level 17. Can use a bonus action to coat an ally's weapon, granting them this bonus for 1 minute.

column4:
Sqeagle are highly valued in the Shiman Federation's industrial sectors and are often found supervising teams of Smudgel in large facilities.
Their bodies have developed a metallic sheen, with portions of their fur becoming steel-like bristles that can scrub away even the most stubborn industrial grime. The tools they carry are extensions of their bodies, forming from concentrated Steel-type ether. Sqeagle take immense pride in their work and the facilities they maintain, often becoming fiercely protective of "their" buildings.
In Venustia and other major cities, Sqeagle supervisors are a common sight at construction sites, factories, and public works facilities. Some noble houses employ Sqeagle as head custodians, trusting them with keys and security responsibilities.
column60: This Pokémon can clean or maintain objects/areas up to three times per day as an action. Each use covers a 20-foot radius. This can remove rust, corrosion, structural weakness (repairs 2d10 hit points to objects/structures). Neutralizes all environmental hazards (toxic spills, magical pollution, etc.). This Pokémon can also identify structural weaknesses within 60 feet
column61:
This Pokémon has Resistance to Normal and Steel-type damage, and gains a +2 AC bonus when defending a location it has maintained for at least one day
Immunity to being moved against its will while standing on ground it has cleaned.
column62:
This Pokémon can direct up to 4 Smudgel or other worker Pokémon as a bonus action.
Allies within 30 feet gain advantage on ability checks related to their work.
Once per long rest, can inspire allies to ignore one level of exhaustion.
